message:
chore(gitignore): cover beads db-shm.bad + root-level recovery dirs

Two ephemeral patterns were leaking into git status from concurrent
beads recovery activity:

- .beads/beads.db-shm.bad_<TS> — corrupted SQLite shared-memory side-
  car file, renamed with a timestamped .bad_ suffix when beads detects
  WAL/SHM mismatch and rebuilds. Existing patterns (*.db, *.db-shm,
  *.db-wal in .beads/.gitignore plus .beads/beads.db.bad_* and
  .beads/beads.db-wal.bad_* at repo root) covered every adjacent
  variant; the db-shm.bad_ flavour was the only gap.
- .beads.recovery_<TS>/ — top-level beads recovery snapshot tree
  (sibling to the repo's .beads/), dropped by the recovery codepath
  to preserve the prior beads state before rebuild. The existing
  .beads.snapshot_*/ pattern only covered the snapshot variant.

Both verified with git check-ignore against the in-tree examples
(.beads/beads.db-shm.bad_20260424T223421Z and
.beads.recovery_20260425T022438Z/). No tracked files affected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
